title: Doellingeria sohayakiensis
intro:
There is insufficient information available about the plant Doellingeria sohayakiensis. However, based on the available data on Doellingeria species, here is a general paragraph about the plant, its uses, and its constituents.

Doellingeria sohayakiensis is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family, Asteraceae. This plant is believed to have various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in reducing inflammation, protecting against cell damage, and preventing infections. The bioactive constituents of Doellingeria sohayakiensis are thought to include sesquiterpenoids and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. However, specific herbal preparations made with Doellingeria sohayakiensis are not well-documented, but it may be used in traditional medicine to make teas, infusions, or decoctions to harness its potential health benefits.
taxonomy: Doellingeria sohayakiensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Doellingeria, which is a group of plants commonly known as false goldenrods. The genus Doellingeria is further classified under the subfamily Asteroideae. The family Asteraceae is a large and diverse group of flowering plants that includes daisies, sunflowers, and many other species. This classification suggests that Doellingeria sohayakiensis is closely related to other plants in the same family and genus.
